title: Daily Briefing — May 01
headline: Tech stumbles, but rotation is alive and well—flows say follow the leaders, not the laggards.
body: Right, so big picture: Equities are pushing highs again, SPY up 1.0% at $718.66, decisively above its 20 MA ($695.26). VIX is drifting lower, now at 17.05 and below its SMA20—volatility is getting crushed as greed keeps climbing (Fear & Greed: 66.7). Tech is mixed: GOOGL rockets (+10%) while NVDA (-4.6%), MSFT (-3.9%), and META (-8.6%) get hammered—showing a clear divergence between mega-cap winners and losers.

Sector rotation is a monster: XLE (Energy), XLB (Materials), XLI (Industrials), XLK (Tech), XLRE (Real Estate) are all in the 'Leading' quadrant, pulling away from the pack. Notably, energy and materials have LOW risk and momentum is positive, while defensives like staples and utilities are only 'improving.' Financials (XLF) are finally above their 20 MA ($51.54), but risk remains 'elevated'—keep an eye on the canary.

Contrarian view: Greed is high but not 'extreme'—we're not at R013 territory yet. The market is frothy, but smarter money isn't stepping out, and bonds (HYG, LQD) are both stable or slightly bull-biased, so no macro alarm bells (R001). But watch for retail chasing tech—NVDA/MSFT selloffs after peak, with GOOGL flying, smells like late-stage FOMO. Don't chase the last move; abundance mindset, look for rotational plays.
callout_body:
- SPY: $718.66 (20 MA: $695.26, bullish above)
- NVDA: $199.57 (watch $196.17, 20 MA support)
- GOOGL: $384.80 (massive momentum, 20 MA: $332.52)
- META: $611.91 (bear bias, below 20 MA: $645.34)
- XLE: $59.65 (20 MA: $57.32)
- XLK: $159.50 (20 MA: $150.36)
- XLF: $52.13 (20 MA: $51.54, must hold for 'canary')
- VIX: 17.05 (signals calm, risk of complacency)
action_items: Watch NVDA, MSFT for price stabilization—don't chase while below 20 MA, but monitor for dark pool flow resets.
Focus on sector rotation: Energy (XLE $59.65), Materials (XLB $51.47), Industrials (XLI $174.58) are strong—look for continuation trades above their 20 MAs.
closing_quote: If it's in the press, it's in the price—flows first, memes later.
